article_title: Cisco Moves to Acquire Astrix Security in Reported $400 Million Deal to Police Non-Human Identities and AI Agents
summary: Duplicate of already-published article on the Cisco/Astrix Security acquisition (PR #1208, merged earlier in this batch). The work is discarded under v3.9.0; not eligible for rewrite.

findings.details: Already-published: src/content/articles/2026-05/10-cisco-to-buy-astrix-security-for-roughly-400-million-to-govern-ai-agents-and-non-human-identities-across-the-enterprise.md (originally PR #1208). Same May 4 2026 announcement, same $400M / $300M figures, same investor list, same product description, same Bailey framing, same integration scope.

content_preview.title: Cisco Moves to Acquire Astrix Security in Reported $400 Million Deal to Police Non-Human Identities and AI Agents
content_preview.summary: The May 4, 2026 announcement folds Astrix's discovery, governance, and threat-detection tooling for API keys, service accounts, and agentic identities into Cisco Identity Intelligence, Secure Access, and Duo IAM.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Cisco announced on May 4, 2026 its intent to acquire Astrix Security, an Israeli startup specializing in non-human identity (NHI) security, [according to SiliconANGLE](https://siliconangle.com/2026/05/04/cisco-buys-astrix-security-strengthen-ai-agent-discovery-governance/). Financial terms were not officially disclosed by Cisco, but [Calcalist reported](https://www.calcalistech.com/ctechnews/article/dy5obf581) the deal value at approximately $400 million, while [SiliconANGLE noted](...

editor_notes.content_quality: Substantively well-written; uses SecurityWeek + Calcalist + SiliconANGLE source set, similar to PR #1208 but with deeper analysis section linking to prior RSAC 2026 and zero-trust coverage. On its own merits would have been APPROVE.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Same Cisco/Astrix acquisition facts as the already-published article.
editor_notes.originality: DUPLICATE of src/content/articles/2026-05/10-cisco-to-buy-astrix-security-for-roughly-400-million-to-govern-ai-agents-and-non-human-identities-across-the-enterprise.md (PR #1208). This is the second of three submissions in this batch on the same announcement (PRs #1208, #1214, #1215).
concerns: Direct duplicate of already-published article. Atomic claim (added in pipeline 3.12.0 after this batch) would have prevented this race-window collision.
recommendations: The atomic-claim mechanism introduced in pipeline 3.12.0 closes this race window for future batches.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: REJECT under v3.9.0. PR #1208 covers this disclosure.
